meta.title: Thumbs Up for Art and Music — Vocabulary Set 4
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: All 16 words in context — sentence completion and word usage practice.

questions.question: She gave a _______ _______ when she agreed with the plan — it means 'yes, I like it!'
questions.explanation: 'Thumbs up' is a gesture or expression of approval. Giving a thumbs up means you agree and support something.

questions.question: _______ class is where students draw, paint, and create original works.
questions.hint: The subject where you create visual things
questions.explanation: 'Art' class is where students develop creative skills — drawing, painting, sculpting, and making original creative works.

questions.question: In _______ class, students sing, play instruments, and learn to read notes.
questions.explanation: 'Music' class covers singing, playing instruments, reading notes, and understanding rhythm. It is a class all about organized sound and performance.

questions.question: I cannot _______ how to solve this puzzle — can you give me a hint?
questions.explanation: 'Figure out' means to find the answer through thinking. 'I can't figure out this puzzle' means I have tried but haven't found the solution yet.

questions.question: I _______ on my math homework and forgot all about dinner.
questions.hint: Focused so hard that you forgot everything else
questions.explanation: 'Concentrated' means focused all attention on one thing. Concentrating so hard on homework that you forget dinner shows complete focus.

questions.question: Her painting of the sunset was _______ — everyone stopped to look at it.
questions.explanation: 'Amazing' means very impressive and wonderful. A painting so beautiful that everyone stops to look is truly amazing — it fills them with wonder and admiration.

questions.question: I have to _______ all the music notes for the concert — there are so many to keep in my head!
questions.hint: Keep in memory; be able to recall later
questions.explanation: 'Remember' means to keep in memory. Remembering music notes means being able to recall them during performance without looking at the sheet.

questions.question: She is very _______. She always thinks of new ideas that nobody else has thought of!
questions.hint: Able to think of new and original ideas
questions.explanation: 'Creative' means able to generate fresh, original ideas. Someone who always thinks of something new that others haven't is described as very creative.

questions.question: The school play required great _______. Every student had a special role and they worked together to make it perfect.
questions.explanation: 'Teamwork' means working together as a group. A school play where everyone has a role and must cooperate is a perfect example of teamwork at its best.

questions.question: Sport is _______ — you have to solve many _______ to do it well, and it teaches you to think!
questions.explanation: 'Important' means significant and valuable. Many skills that seem unrelated (like sport) are important because they teach thinking, problem-solving, and discipline.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence from the text:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Art helps you be creative.' This is one of the key claims in the text — art develops your creativity.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses BOTH 'concentrate' and 'remember' correctly:
options: She concentrateed to remember the music.
If you concentrate while learning, you will remember the information better.
Remember to concentrate a music fun.
questions.explanation: 'If you concentrate' (focus your attention) 'you will remember better' (recall it more easily later). This is a true learning principle — focused attention improves memory.

questions.question: The festival was _______ — the music, dancing, and art displays were all incredible!
questions.explanation: 'Amazing' = very impressive and wonderful. A festival with incredible music, dancing, and art displays would definitely be amazing — you'd say 'Wow!' about everything.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about creativity:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'You use your imagination to be creative.' This explains the relationship: imagination (the ability to picture new ideas) is what allows you to BE creative.

questions.question: In the text, 'math problems' means art and music create difficulties in math class.
questions.explanation: No! 'Math problems' means math exercises and questions — not difficulties. The text says art and music help you get BETTER at math problems, not that they cause problems.
